title: Cerinthodes simplicissimum
taxonomy: Cerinthodes simplicissimum is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is a member of the genus Cerinthodes, which is closely related to the genus Wahlenbergia. The Campanulaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as bellflowers. Cerinthodes simplicissimum is native to New Guinea, where it can be found growing in humid, subtropical environments. Its classification as a species within the Campanulaceae family reflects its unique characteristics and shared traits with other members of this group.
